memberdef.name: OS_MAX_BLOCK_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 20480 /* Maximum size per allocation - INCREASED for MboxPostMsg (16432 bytes) */

briefdescription.para: Allocate a continuous block of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Size of memory block to be allocated

briefdescription.para: Free a continuous block of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to previously allocated memory block

briefdescription.para: Get memory pool statistics.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to store number of used blocks
Pointer to store number of free blocks
Pointer to store total memory size
Pointer to store used memory size

briefdescription.para: Print memory pool statistics (debug function)
Verify memory pool initialization and perform basic tests.

simplesect.para: OSLAYER_OK on success, OSLAYER_ERROR on failure

briefdescription.para: Opens a file using the specified filename and mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function allocates and initializes an osFile structure, opens the file with the given filename and mode, and associates the resulting FILE/FIL* with the osFile structure.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the file to be opened.
The mode in which the file is to be opened (e.g., "r", "w").

simplesect.para: A pointer to the opened osFile structure, or NULL if an error occurred during file opening or memory allocation.

briefdescription.para: Closes the file associated with the given osFile structure.

detaileddescription.para: This function closes the file associated with the given osFile structure, frees the allocated memory for the structure, and performs necessary cleanup.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

briefdescription.para: Read data from a file into a specified buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads data from a file associated with the given osFile structure into the specified buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the buffer where the read data will be stored.
Size of each data item to be read (in bytes).
Number of data items to read.
Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Number of data items actually read. If the return value is less than count, it may indicate reaching the end of the file or encountering an error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *ptr, size_t size, size_t count, osFile *pVsiFile)
memberdef.name: osFwrite

briefdescription.para: Write data from a specified buffer to a file.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes data from the specified buffer into the file associated with the given osFile structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the buffer containing the data to be written to the file.
Size of each data item to be written (in bytes).
Number of data items to write.
Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Number of data items actually written to the file. If the return value is less than count, it may indicate disk full or encountering an error.

briefdescription.para: Set the file position indicator for the specified file.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the file position indicator for the file associated with the given osFile structure. The new position is determined by adding offset bytes to the position specified by origin.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.
Number of bytes to offset from origin.
Position from where offset is added (SEEK_SET, SEEK_CUR, SEEK_END).

briefdescription.para: Get the current file position indicator for the specified file.

detaileddescription.para: This function gets the current file position indicator for the file associated with the given osFile structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Current file position indicator if successful, -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Get the size of the file associated with the specified osFile structure.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the size of the file associated with the given osFile structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Size of the file if successful, -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Check if the end-of-file indicator is set for the specified file.

detaileddescription.para: This function checks whether the end-of-file indicator is set for the file associated with the given osFile structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Non-zero value if the end-of-file indicator is set, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if an error indicator is set for the specified file.

detaileddescription.para: This function checks whether an error indicator is set for the file associated with the given osFile structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Non-zero value if an error indicator is set, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Read a character from the specified file.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads a character from the file associated with the given osFile structure. It returns when either a character is read, the end-of-file is reached, or an error occurs, whichever comes first.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Integer of the read character on success, -1 on failure or end-of-file.

briefdescription.para: Read a line from the specified file into the specified buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads a line from the file associated with the given osFile structure into the specified buffer. It stops when either (n-1) characters are read, the newline character is encountered, or the end-of-file is reached, whichever comes first. The newline character, if encountered, is included in the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the buffer where the line will be stored.
Maximum number of characters to be read (including the null terminator).
Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the read line on success, NULL on failure or end-of-file.

briefdescription.para: Read a line from the specified file into the specified buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes a string to the file associated with the given osFile structure

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the string which will be written into the file.
Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.

briefdescription.para: Read formatted data from the specified file.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads formatted data from the file associated with the given osFile structure, under the control of the format string format. The variable arguments must correspond to the placeholders in the format string.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.
Format string that specifies the expected format of the input.
Variable arguments corresponding to the placeholders in the format string.

simplesect.para: Number of input items successfully matched and assigned, or EOF if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: Write formatted data to the specified file.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes formatted data to the file associated with the given osFile structure, under the control of the format string format. The variable arguments must correspond to the placeholders in the format string.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the osFile structure representing the file.
Format string that specifies the expected format of the output.
Variable arguments corresponding to the placeholders in the format string.

simplesect.para: Number of characters written, or a negative value if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: Sleeps the program for the specified number of microseconds.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to make the program sleep for the specified number of microseconds.

parameterdescription.para: The number of microseconds to sleep.

briefdescription.para: Returns a 64-bit timestamp [us].

parameterdescription.para: Reference of the timestamp object
Event successfully created

briefdescription.para: Returns a 64-bit timestamp [ns].

parameterdescription.para: Reference of the timestamp object
Event successfully created
